seo.title: Healthcare AI Chatbot AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for ai chatbot AI agents in healthcare. Covers HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, HITECH Act.

content.intro: Deploying an AI chatbot in healthcare environments carries critical risks including patient harm, HIPAA violations, and malpractice liability that demand rigorous pre-deployment validation. This checklist addresses the unique intersection of conversational AI architecture — including RAG pipelines, multi-turn memory, and intent classification — with healthcare-specific compliance requirements under HIPAA, HITECH, FDA 21 CFR Part 11, and SOC 2 Type II. Every item is designed to be actionable before your chatbot touches a single patient interaction.

sections.heading: PHI Protection & Data Governance
sections.description: Ensure Protected Health Information is never exposed to external LLM APIs, is de-identified before processing, and that all data flows comply with HIPAA minimum necessary standards.

items.label: PHI De-identification Pipeline Validation
items.description: Implement and validate an automated de-identification layer (per HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination method) that strips all 18 PHI identifiers before any text reaches the LLM inference layer. Run test suites with synthetic PHI to confirm zero leakage.

items.label: Data Residency Enforcement
items.description: Confirm that all patient conversation data, RAG vector embeddings, and retrieved documents are stored and processed exclusively within your HIPAA-compliant environment. Disable any LLM provider telemetry, logging, or training data opt-ins that could transmit data externally.

items.label: Multi-Turn Memory Encryption & Scoping
items.description: Encrypt conversation memory buffers at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3). Enforce strict session scoping so that memory from one patient session cannot bleed into another, and implement automatic memory purge after session expiration per your data retention policy.

items.label: RAG Knowledge Base Access Controls
items.description: Apply role-based access controls to the retrieval corpus so the chatbot only retrieves documents the authenticated user is authorized to access. Audit retrieval logs to detect unauthorized cross-patient document access.

items.label: Business Associate Agreement (BAA) Verification
items.description: Confirm signed BAAs are in place with every third-party vendor in the AI stack (cloud provider, vector database, monitoring tools). Document BAA coverage in your risk register and review annually.

items.label: Data Minimization in Prompt Construction
items.description: Audit prompt templates to ensure only the minimum necessary context is injected into LLM prompts. Remove any fields from retrieved documents that contain PHI not required for the specific query intent.

items.label: Breach Detection & Notification Workflow
items.description: Implement real-time monitoring for anomalous data access patterns in the chatbot pipeline. Define and test a documented breach notification workflow that meets HITECH's 60-day notification requirement for covered entities.

sections.heading: Clinical Safety & Response Accuracy
sections.description: Validate that the chatbot provides clinically accurate, safe responses and never substitutes for licensed medical judgment, with hallucination detection and mandatory escalation paths.

items.label: Hallucination Detection & Confidence Thresholding
items.description: Integrate a hallucination detection layer that cross-references LLM responses against retrieved source documents. Set confidence thresholds below which the chatbot must decline to answer and escalate to a human clinician rather than generating an unsupported response.

items.label: Clinical Escalation Trigger Mapping
items.description: Define and implement a comprehensive list of intent triggers (e.g., suicidal ideation, chest pain, medication overdose queries) that immediately route the conversation to a licensed clinician or emergency services. Test all triggers with adversarial inputs monthly.

items.label: Medical Disclaimer & Scope Boundary Enforcement
items.description: Enforce hard-coded response guardrails that prevent the chatbot from providing diagnoses, prescribing medications, or interpreting lab results. Every response touching clinical topics must include a validated disclaimer and recommend professional consultation.

items.label: Drug Interaction & Contraindication Safety Filter
items.description: If the chatbot handles medication-related queries, integrate a validated drug interaction database check before any medication information is surfaced. Flag and suppress responses where retrieved content conflicts with known contraindications for the patient's profile.

items.label: Response Accuracy Benchmarking Against Clinical Standards
items.description: Conduct pre-deployment accuracy testing using a curated dataset of clinical Q&A pairs validated by licensed clinicians. Establish a minimum accuracy threshold (e.g., 95% alignment with clinical guidelines) and block deployment if not met.

items.label: Conversation Coherence Testing for Multi-Turn Scenarios
items.description: Run structured multi-turn conversation test suites that simulate realistic patient journeys (symptom inquiry → triage → follow-up). Validate that context is maintained accurately across turns and that prior context does not introduce clinical contradictions.

items.label: Out-of-Scope Intent Rejection Testing
items.description: Test the intent classifier against a library of out-of-scope queries (legal advice, financial guidance, non-health topics) to confirm reliable rejection and appropriate redirection without exposing sensitive system prompts.

sections.heading: Auditability & Regulatory Compliance
sections.description: Implement immutable audit trails, electronic record controls, and documentation required by FDA 21 CFR Part 11, HIPAA, and SOC 2 Type II to ensure every AI-assisted interaction is fully auditable.

items.label: Immutable Audit Log Implementation
items.description: Deploy append-only, tamper-evident audit logs capturing every chatbot interaction: user ID, timestamp, input query (de-identified), retrieved documents, LLM response, and any escalation events. Logs must be stored separately from the application database and retained per your HIPAA retention policy (minimum 6 years).

items.label: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 Electronic Record Controls
items.description: If the chatbot generates records used in clinical decision-making or trials, implement 21 CFR Part 11-compliant electronic signatures, audit trails, and system access controls. Document validation protocols (IQ/OQ/PQ) for the chatbot system.

items.label: AI Decision Traceability Documentation
items.description: For every chatbot response that influences a clinical workflow, log the full reasoning chain: retrieved document sources, relevance scores, prompt version used, and model version. This traceability package must be producible within 24 hours for regulatory audit.

items.label: SOC 2 Type II Control Mapping
items.description: Map chatbot system controls to your SOC 2 Type II trust service criteria (Security, Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy). Ensure the chatbot is included in your annual SOC 2 audit scope and that evidence collection is automated.

items.label: Model Version Control & Change Management
items.description: Implement strict version control for LLM model versions, prompt templates, RAG index versions, and intent classifier models. Any change to these components must trigger a documented change management review and regression testing before production deployment.

items.label: User Consent Logging for AI Interaction
items.description: Capture and store explicit patient consent for AI-assisted interactions at session initiation. Log consent version, timestamp, and patient identifier (in your secure environment) to demonstrate informed consent in the event of a regulatory inquiry.

items.label: Periodic Compliance Audit Scheduling
items.description: Schedule quarterly internal audits of chatbot audit logs, access controls, and PHI handling procedures. Assign a named compliance officer responsible for reviewing audit findings and remediating gaps within defined SLAs.

sections.heading: Operational Safety & Incident Response
sections.description: Establish monitoring, safety filtering, and incident response procedures to detect and contain failures before they result in patient harm or regulatory violations.

items.label: Real-Time PII Detection in Outputs
items.description: Deploy an output-layer PII scanner that inspects every chatbot response before delivery to the user. If PHI or PII is detected in the output (e.g., another patient's data surfaced via RAG), the response must be blocked, logged, and flagged for immediate human review.

items.label: Prompt Injection Attack Prevention
items.description: Implement input sanitization and prompt injection detection to prevent adversarial users from manipulating the chatbot into bypassing safety filters, revealing system prompts, or accessing unauthorized patient records. Test with a library of known prompt injection patterns monthly.

items.label: Safety Filter Coverage Validation
items.description: Validate that content safety filters block harmful outputs including self-harm instructions, dangerous medication dosage guidance, and discriminatory clinical advice. Use red-team testing with clinical safety experts to identify filter gaps before go-live.

items.label: Incident Response Playbook for AI Failures
items.description: Document and rehearse a specific incident response playbook for AI chatbot failures including: clinical misinformation delivered to a patient, PHI breach via chatbot, and chatbot unavailability during critical care workflows. Define RTO/RPO targets and escalation contacts.

items.label: Anomaly Detection & Alerting on Conversation Patterns
items.description: Implement behavioral monitoring that alerts on anomalous conversation patterns such as unusually high volume of escalation triggers, repeated failed intent classifications, or sudden spikes in out-of-scope queries that may indicate misuse or system compromise.

items.label: Clinician Override & Correction Mechanism
items.description: Provide licensed clinicians with a documented, audited mechanism to flag, correct, and annotate incorrect chatbot responses. Corrections must feed back into a supervised fine-tuning or RAG knowledge base update workflow with clinical review approval.

items.label: Downtime & Fallback Protocol for Clinical Continuity
items.description: Define a fallback protocol for chatbot downtime that ensures patients are immediately routed to human support channels. Post clear fallback instructions within the chat interface and test failover procedures quarterly.

items.label: Staff Training on AI Limitations & Escalation Duties
items.description: Require documented training for all clinical and administrative staff who interact with or supervise the chatbot. Training must cover AI hallucination risks, mandatory escalation scenarios, and how to report suspected AI errors through your incident management system.

regulatoryNotes.heading: Regulatory Considerations
notes: HIPAA & HITECH Enforcement: The HHS Office for Civil Rights can impose tiered penalties up to $1.5M per violation category per year. For AI chatbots, the highest risk violations are impermissible PHI disclosures through LLM API calls to non-BAA-covered vendors and failure to implement technical safeguards for PHI in transit and at rest within the chatbot pipeline.

FDA 21 CFR Part 11 Applicability: If your AI chatbot creates, modifies, or transmits electronic records used in FDA-regulated clinical activities (e.g., clinical trials, adverse event reporting), the system must meet 21 CFR Part 11 requirements including audit trails, electronic signatures, and validated system controls. Consult your regulatory affairs team to determine if your chatbot's outputs constitute regulated electronic records.

FDA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) Classification: AI chatbots that provide clinical decision support — particularly those that analyze patient symptoms and recommend diagnoses or treatments — may be classified as Software as a Medical Device under FDA guidance. Review the FDA's 2023 AI/ML-Based SaMD Action Plan and determine whether your chatbot requires 510(k) clearance or falls under the clinical decision support software exemption.

SOC 2 Type II Scope Inclusion: Your AI chatbot infrastructure — including the LLM inference environment, RAG vector database, conversation memory store, and audit logging system — must be explicitly included in your SOC 2 Type II audit scope. Ensure your auditor has reviewed the AI-specific controls and that your trust service criteria mappings address the unique risks of generative AI outputs and retrieval-augmented architectures.

cta.heading: Automate Your Healthcare Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your healthcare AI chatbot against HIPAA, HITECH, and FDA requirements — catching compliance gaps before they become $1.5M violations.
